evals.name: pre-handoff-pass-case
evals.prompt: I'm about to PATCH issue TEC-2001 from in_progress to in_review. Working tree at /tmp/repo, currently on branch feature/TEC-2001-add-export. git log shows one commit on this branch with message 'TEC-2001: add CSV export endpoint'. for-each-ref lists refs/heads/feature/TEC-2001-add-export. Should I run any pre-handoff check, and what is the expected outcome?
evals.expected_output: Should locate the 'Pre-handoff branch + commit verification' section. Should walk through the bash block: NEEDLE='tec-2001'; for-each-ref grep matches refs/heads/feature/TEC-2001-add-export (case-insensitive, non-digit boundary holds); git log --grep='TEC-2001' on that ref returns the commit SHA. Both checks pass. Conclude: PASS — proceed with PATCH to in_review.
assertions: Identifies and runs the Pre-handoff branch + commit verification check
Lowercases the identifier to 'tec-2001' for the regex needle
Confirms branch match via for-each-ref grep
Confirms commit match via git log --grep
Concludes PASS and authorises the in_review PATCH

evals.name: pre-handoff-fail-no-branch
evals.prompt: I'm about to PATCH TEC-2002 to in_review. Working tree at /tmp/repo. for-each-ref output: refs/heads/main, refs/heads/feature/TEC-1999-something-else, refs/remotes/origin/main. The workspace still exists. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Should run the verification, lowercase to 'tec-2002', grep for-each-ref output and find no match (only TEC-1999 and main refs exist; non-digit boundary rule prevents tec-1999/main from matching tec-2002). Should report 'FAIL: no branch references TEC-2002'. Should NOT instruct the IC to PATCH in_review. Per the on-fail table: workspace exists → commit work onto a TEC-2002-named branch, push, then re-run the check.
assertions: Runs the verification and detects no matching branch
Returns the FAIL message naming the missing identifier
Refuses to PATCH the issue to in_review
Instructs commit + push onto a TEC-2002-named branch and re-run
Does not falsely match TEC-1999 or main as a TEC-2002 branch

evals.name: pre-handoff-fail-no-commit
evals.prompt: I'm about to PATCH TEC-2003 to in_review. Branch feature/TEC-2003-foo exists locally and on origin. But git log feature/TEC-2003-foo --grep='TEC-2003' -i returns nothing — the branch was created but no commits reference the identifier. Workspace exists. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Should run the check: branch match succeeds via for-each-ref. Then git log --grep on the matched ref returns empty, so the second check fails with 'FAIL: branch feature/TEC-2003-foo found but no commit references TEC-2003'. Should NOT PATCH in_review. Should add a commit that references TEC-2003 in the message (or amend an existing one if appropriate), push, then re-run the check.
assertions: Confirms the first check (branch match) passes
Detects the second check (commit match) fails
Returns the FAIL message naming both the branch and the missing identifier
Refuses to PATCH the issue to in_review
Instructs adding a commit that references TEC-2003 and pushing

evals.name: pre-handoff-bypass-plan-only
evals.prompt: I'm finishing TEC-2004, which is a plan-only issue. The deliverable is the issue's plan document. No working tree was ever opened for this issue, no branch exists, no code changes were made. I'm about to reassign back to the requester (Dev Lead) for plan review. Do I need to run the pre-handoff branch+commit check?
evals.expected_output: Should consult the Bypass cases list and identify that this is BOTH a plan-only/research issue (no working tree) AND a hand-off back to a non-QA reviewer (Dev Lead/requester). Either bypass alone is sufficient. Should NOT run the git check. Should proceed with the in_review PATCH and reassign to the Dev Lead. Should briefly cite the bypass reason (plan-only, no working tree) so the audit trail is clear.
assertions: Recognises the bypass cases section applies
Identifies plan-only / no-working-tree as a valid bypass
Identifies routing-back-to-requester (non-QA) as a separately valid bypass
Skips the git verification check
Proceeds with the in_review PATCH and notes the bypass reason

evals.name: pre-handoff-local-only-repo-carve-out
evals.prompt: I am handing off TEC-2005 from in_progress to in_review. Workspace exists at /tmp/repo and this repo is intentionally local-only (no origin configured; no push target by design). for-each-ref does not include a branch with TEC-2005 in its name, but my local HEAD includes commit 9f8e7d6 with message 'TEC-2005: implement parser hardening'. What does the pre-handoff gate require?
evals.expected_output: Should run/assess the pre-handoff check and identify the first condition as nominally failing (no branch references TEC-2005). Then apply the local-only repo carve-out from the on-fail table: this is non-blocking when the repo has no origin/push target by design and there is a local commit in the active working tree referencing TEC-2005. Should require a checkpoint note that the repo is local-only and cite commit 9f8e7d6 before handoff. Should not instruct creating/pushing a branch when push is impossible by design.
assertions: Recognises the no-branch condition
Applies the local-only repo carve-out instead of hard-failing
Requires explicit checkpoint note that repo is local-only
Requires citing a local commit SHA that references the identifier
Does not require a push/origin action for the carve-out path

evals.name: shared-working-tree-worktree-trigger
evals.prompt: I am agent TEC-2006-agent working on issue TEC-2006. My intended branch is feature/TEC-2006-foo. PAPERCLIP_EXECUTION_WORKSPACE_ID is null. I run git status and see: 'On branch fix/tec-997-bootstrap-icons-font'. The working tree has no uncommitted changes. I need to edit src/components/Header.tsx for my task. What should I do before making any edits?
evals.expected_output: Should identify the 'Shared working-tree protocol' section, specifically rule 2: when PAPERCLIP_EXECUTION_WORKSPACE_ID is null AND git status shows another agent's branch checked out (fix/tec-997-bootstrap-icons-font is not feature/TEC-2006-foo), the agent MUST add a per-issue worktree before any code edit. Should instruct: git worktree add -b feature/TEC-2006-foo ~/.paperclip/worktrees/TEC-2006/ <base-ref>, then cd ~/.paperclip/worktrees/TEC-2006/. Should NOT proceed with edits in the current checkout. Should reference the superpowers:using-git-worktrees skill for full mechanics. The worktree path is keyed on the Paperclip issue identifier so concurrent issues never collide.
assertions: Identifies PAPERCLIP_EXECUTION_WORKSPACE_ID as null, triggering the shared-checkout check
Detects that the current branch (fix/tec-997-bootstrap-icons-font) is not the intended branch (feature/TEC-2006-foo)
Requires creating a per-issue worktree before any code edit per rule 2
Uses the correct worktree path keyed on the issue identifier (~/.paperclip/worktrees/TEC-2006/)
Does NOT proceed with edits in the existing checkout

evals.name: shared-working-tree-branch-identity-hard-stop
evals.prompt: I am about to run git commit on branch feature/TEC-2007-dashboard. Per the shared working-tree protocol rule 3, I run the branch-identity check immediately before committing. EXPECTED_BRANCH is 'feature/TEC-2007-dashboard'. I execute: ACTUAL_BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) and it returns 'fix/tec-997-bootstrap-icons-font'. EXPECTED_BRANCH != ACTUAL_BRANCH. What must I do?
evals.expected_output: Should identify the 'Branch-identity check around every commit and push' section (rule 3 of the shared working-tree protocol). The mismatch (EXPECTED_BRANCH='feature/TEC-2007-dashboard' vs ACTUAL_BRANCH='fix/tec-997-bootstrap-icons-font') is a hard stop. The agent MUST emit the ABORT message: 'ABORT: HEAD is on fix/tec-997-bootstrap-icons-font, expected feature/TEC-2007-dashboard' and exit without committing. Critically, the agent must NOT attempt to check out the expected branch to 'fix' the mismatch, because that would throw away whatever the other agent staged. This is a hard stop — do not proceed, do not self-correct the branch.
assertions: Identifies the branch-identity check mismatch as a hard stop per rule 3
Emits the ABORT message naming both the actual and expected branches
Does NOT commit — exits without any mutating git operation
Does NOT attempt to git checkout or git switch to the expected branch to fix the mismatch
Treats the mismatch as unrecoverable in this heartbeat — does not attempt workarounds

evals.name: cross-agent-contamination-recovery-full
evals.prompt: I am working on issue TEC-2006. My intended branch is feature/TEC-2006-foo. I discover that commit abc1234 with message 'TEC-2006: add export handler' landed on fix/other-branch instead of feature/TEC-2006-foo. git log fix/other-branch shows abc1234 but git log feature/TEC-2006-foo does not. Furthermore, abc1234 is already on origin/fix/other-branch (it was pushed). The Paperclip issue that owns fix/other-branch's PR is TEC-3050, and the PR reviewer is @qa-engineer. Walk me through the full recovery.
evals.expected_output: Should identify the 'Cross-agent contamination recovery' section and walk through all five steps. Step 1 — Detect: compare git log on both branches, confirm abc1234 appears on fix/other-branch but not feature/TEC-2006-foo, and note that abc1234 is also on origin/fix/other-branch so origin contamination must also be addressed. Step 2 — Cherry-pick: git switch feature/TEC-2006-foo, git cherry-pick abc1234, git push origin feature/TEC-2006-foo — resolve conflicts normally, do not skip the cherry-pick. Step 3 — Revert on fix/other-branch: git switch fix/other-branch, git revert abc1234 --no-edit, git push origin fix/other-branch — do not force-push, use revert. Step 4 — Disclose on TEC-3050: post a comment on the Paperclip issue that owns fix/other-branch's PR (TEC-3050) describing the contaminating SHA (abc1234), what it contained (TEC-2006 export handler), which branch/PR it contaminated (fix/other-branch), which branch it now lives on (feature/TEC-2006-foo), the revert SHA on fix/other-branch, and an @qa-engineer mention so they re-check the diff before merging. Step 5 — Stash hygiene: note that stashes are global to the repo; list stashes with git stash list, inspect with git stash show -p stash@{N} before applying, and only pop/apply if the diff matches expectations — leave unfamiliar stashes alone.
assertions: Walks through step 1 (detect): compares git log on both branches, identifies abc1234 as misrouted and confirms origin contamination
Walks through step 2 (cherry-pick): switches to feature/TEC-2006-foo, cherry-picks abc1234, pushes — does not skip the cherry-pick
Walks through step 3 (revert): switches to fix/other-branch, reverts abc1234, pushes — uses revert not force-push
Walks through step 4 (disclose): posts on TEC-3050 with the contaminating SHA, affected branch/PR, revert SHA, and @qa-engineer mention
Walks through step 5 (stash hygiene): notes stashes are global, instructs inspecting stash diffs before applying, leaves unfamiliar stashes alone
